changes.before: Somebody had put the thing where everyone would swallow it willingly.
changes.after: Somebody had buried the taint in bread everyone would eat willingly.

changes.before: It carries the thing the rest of the way until death finishes it.
changes.after: It carries the taint the rest of the way until death finishes it.

changes.before: "Truth first. Judgment after, if there's time."
changes.after: "Say what it is first. Curse it after, if there's time."

changes.before: That was cleaner than the fight Jaina had been having with herself.
The rest of her meaning was clear enough.
Patience in Sylvanas meant she thought the outcome settled and was waiting for the proof to catch up.
changes.after: The pause after that held Sylvanas in her usual patient stillness, the one she wore when she thought an argument was finished.
He never interrupted when the answer interested him.

changes.before: The grain was still moving. The city was still working.
changes.after: The grain was still moving through a city that still worked.

changes.before: A prince would ask for records. Soldiers in camp would wait for orders.
changes.after: A prince would ask for records; soldiers in camp would wait for orders.
The gates were close enough now that, with her wards pulled tight, Jaina could ride straight toward them.
